reference: Principle: Take your time and think
text: Take your time and think — Rushed decisions often lead to regret. Create space for reflection.

reference: Principle: Clarity > priority > focus > action
text: Clarity > priority > focus > action — First understand clearly, then prioritize, then focus, then act.

reference: Principle: Focus on what you can control
text: Focus on what you can control — Distinguish between what is within your power and what is not.

reference: Principle: Consider second-order effects
text: Consider second-order effects — Think about the consequences of consequences.

reference: Principle: You can do anything, but not everything at once
text: You can do anything, but not everything at once — Choose deliberately what to focus on now.

reference: Principle: Small consistent steps compound
text: Small consistent steps compound — Daily 1% improvements lead to 37x growth in a year.

reference: Principle: Action > patience > learn > win
text: Action > patience > learn > win — Start with action, be patient with results, learn from feedback, eventually succeed.

reference: Principle: Energy management over time management
text: Energy management over time management — Protect your energy; time without energy is wasted.

reference: Principle: Name it to tame it
text: Name it to tame it — Labeling emotions reduces their intensity.

reference: Principle: Respond, don't react
text: Respond, don't react — Create a gap between stimulus and response.

reference: Principle: This too shall pass
text: This too shall pass — Both good and bad states are temporary.

reference: Principle: Feelings are data, not directives
text: Feelings are data, not directives — Emotions provide information but don't require immediate action.

reference: Principle: Seek first to understand, then to be understood
text: Seek first to understand, then to be understood — Listen before speaking.

reference: Principle: Assume positive intent
text: Assume positive intent — Most people aren't trying to harm you.

reference: Principle: You can be right or you can be happy
text: You can be right or you can be happy — Sometimes connection matters more than correctness.

reference: Principle: Boundaries are kindness
text: Boundaries are kindness — Clear limits enable sustainable relationships.

reference: Principle: Embrace discomfort as growth
text: Embrace discomfort as growth — Growth happens at the edge of your comfort zone.

reference: Principle: Fail fast, learn faster
text: Fail fast, learn faster — Quick experiments with rapid feedback accelerate learning.

reference: Principle: Compare yourself to who you were yesterday
text: Compare yourself to who you were yesterday — The only meaningful comparison is with your past self.

reference: Principle: What got you here won't get you there
text: What got you here won't get you there — New levels require new behaviors.
